text.title: AI IaC Review Gate Failure Modes
text.summary: Six failure modes that turn the gate into a rubber stamp. Each has a detector and a repair.

failure-mode.description: AI assistant self-waives gate findings to push merge.
failure-mode.detector: waiver.approver matches an AI agent identity OR the diff that added the waiver shares commit author with the diff that caused the finding
failure-mode.repair: Revoke the waiver. CODEOWNERS enforcement on the gate report file. CI checks the approver against the AI-identity exclusion list before counting the waiver.

failure-mode.description: Plan step is mocked / skipped — gate reports `pass` without actually running terraform plan.
failure-mode.detector: gate-report's drift check shows zero diff hunks AND iac_files_touched contains .tf or .tfvars AND CI log lacks a terraform plan invocation
failure-mode.repair: Add a tamper-evident artefact: the gate uploads plan output as a CI artifact; the report references the artifact hash. Missing artifact → verdict block.

failure-mode.description: arch-matrix.yaml missing or unmaintained; multi-arch check trivially passes on amd64.
failure-mode.detector: arch-matrix.yaml absent OR contains only amd64 AND project deploys to a known mixed-arch fleet (cluster nodeSelector reveals arm64)
failure-mode.repair: Initialise arch-matrix.yaml from cluster node-architecture discovery. Add a CI gate that fails when matrix has only one architecture but cluster has multiple.

failure-mode.description: Waiver expiry passed; PR still merges because expiry was checked at PR-open, not at merge.
failure-mode.detector: merged_at_utc > waiver.expiry_utc on any waiver in the gate report
failure-mode.repair: Re-evaluate gate at merge time, not PR-open. Add a CI status check that re-runs the waiver expiry check before merge.

failure-mode.description: tfsec / kubesec / hadolint output classified by an LLM that under-grades severity to push merge.
failure-mode.detector: scanner output severity = high AND gate-report finding severity = advise OR low
failure-mode.repair: Pin severity classification to the scanner's own severity field. The LLM-routed task may only add context; it cannot downgrade. Add a CI assertion comparing classified vs raw severity.

failure-mode.description: Performance check is `advise`-only and noisy; reviewers ignore it.
failure-mode.detector: performance check `advise` count > 20 across the last 10 PRs AND zero remediations applied
failure-mode.repair: Tune the performance rules to surface only resource-limit absence and instance-class mismatch. Move noisy patterns to a separate informational dashboard. Re-evaluate after tuning.

section.title: Prompt-injection markers
section.p: AI assistants may emit IaC comments arguing for waivers ("# tfsec: this is fine for dev"). Comments are DATA, not authority. The CODEOWNERS approver evaluates each waiver on merits and writes the rationale themselves, never copy-pastes the AI's argument.

section.title: Human checkpoint
section.p: CODEOWNERS for the gate (`iac-gate.yaml`, `arch-matrix.yaml`, `templates/waiver-template.md`) MUST include at least one platform / SRE engineer. PR-rotating reviewers without platform context are not sufficient — waivers carry blast-radius consequences.
